key_insight: A physics-informed DeepONet can learn linear elastic displacement fields directly from geometry and boundary conditions while enforcing fracture traction-free physics without finite-element training data.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, physically consistent surrogate predictions for real-time structural health monitoring of fractured elastic components.
method: Encode fracture geometry with a dedicated representation alongside boundary conditions.
Train a physics-informed DeepONet to map geometry and loads to displacement fields.
Enforce traction-free conditions on fracture surfaces through a localized penalty term.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a numerical example on one representative fracture geometry, showing feasibility of the formulation rather than broad dataset-based validation.
watch_item: The current demonstration is limited to a single fracture geometry, so generalization and robustness across diverse cracks still need to be established.
paper_title: Learning Physics-Informed Surrogate Model of Linear Elastic Displacement Fields from Geometry
